tables.title: OTHER FEES
rows: Payable monthly, by the 6th day of the month
Paid directly from Gross Revenue; see Gross Revenue definition in Note 2.
Payable monthly, by the 6th day of the month
Used for Brand Fund. Franchisor has the right to adjust; expects to raise to 2% of Gross Revenue in 2026.
As incurred by you; measured yearly

Minimum local spend; monthly accounting required within 30 days of month end; franchisor may require you to spend through specific suppliers or to pay the amount to franchisor/affiliate for use on your behalf.
As determined by cooperative; currently none; maximum 2% of Gross Revenues
Payable monthly, by the 6th day of the month
Franchisor may require membership; each member (franchised or company-owned) has one vote on the council.
Pass-through of costs, plus reasonable administrative charge. Currently none.
Franchisor may require use of designated third-party suppliers (technology, payroll, real estate, construction management, customer feedback); franchisor may collect and charge a markup.
Franchisor and affiliates reserve the right to become approved or exclusive supplier of any product or service.
Out-of-pocket costs plus internal cost allocation, plus 10%
Franchisor may cure non-compliance on franchisee's behalf (e.g., purchase insurance, conduct refurbishment) and charge costs plus 10% admin fee.
Franchisor may take any action it deems appropriate to resolve a customer complaint; franchisee must reimburse expenses.
All costs and expenses associated with audit, approximately $1,500 to $5,000
Payable if franchisor audits due to failure to submit reports or other non-compliance, or if audit concludes Gross Revenues were under-reported by more than 3% for any month.
Payable if franchisor conducts inspection due to government report, customer complaint, or default.
1.5% per month or highest rate allowed by state (whichever is lower)
Applies to all overdue fees and understatements revealed by audit.
Approval of Products, Services, or Suppliers
Applies if, at franchisee request, franchisor evaluates a potential new supplier or product/service.
Payable to franchisor upon transfer of business.
Paid by the transferee to cover training costs.
Greater of (a) $10,000 or (b) 10% of the initial franchise fee then in effect for new franchisees
Payable if franchisee exercises right to renew per 10-year renewal term.
Reimbursement of franchisor's costs if franchisor agrees to assist relocation.
Substitute or New School Administrator Training
Currently $500 per week for attendance at Ivybrook University or similar
If School Administrator is replaced after opening, replacement must attend training.
Franchisor's then-current charge; currently $500 per day plus expenses
Beyond what franchisor already provides; travel expenses added if franchisor must travel.
All costs including reasonable attorneys' fees
Reimbursement for costs in enforcing obligations or costs arising out of default/termination.
All costs, losses, and expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees incurred as result of defaults
Reimbursement for all costs and damages from franchisee defaults.
Any and all types of damages, liabilities, losses, costs, and expenses from third-party claims or franchisee operation
Franchisee, owners, and guarantors must indemnify franchisor and related parties.
May be used for any technology-related purpose; franchisor may increase at any time during the term. Currently covers intranet, website, and other franchisor-provided technology.
Franchisee attendance required at franchisor conferences; travel/lodging at franchisee expense; fee charged for non-attendance.
Greater of $1,000 or 150% of the Royalty due for the same week for every week of month-to-month operations, up to the then-current initial franchise fee
Applies only if franchisee continues operating after expiration of franchise agreement.
Varies; for ongoing non-compliance, up to $1,000 per week plus costs and expenses
Franchisor may charge fee per non-compliance instance.
If franchisee fails to timely provide requested or required information.

Varies; monthly average of Royalty (without fee waivers/reductions) owed during the 12 months preceding the effective date of termination, multiplied by the number of months remaining in the term of the Franchise Agreement
Within 15 days after termination of the Franchise Agreement
Payable if franchisor terminates for cause.
If franchisee or owners engage in a transfer without complying with Franchise Agreement terms.
All costs and expenses associated with System modification
If franchisor makes System changes (e.g., new equipment or software); payments may be to affiliates or third-party suppliers.
table_notes: Note 1: All fees are payable to franchisor; all fees are imposed and collected by franchisor; all fees are non-refundable; all fees are uniform for all franchisees, although franchisor reserves the right to change, waive, or eliminate fees for any franchisee; all fees are payable by electronic debit or other method franchisor determines.

Note 2: 'Gross Revenues' means all revenue generated from the Franchised Business and from any operations at the accepted location, whether for check, cash, credit, or otherwise, including proceeds from business interruption insurance, but excluding (a) any revenue refunded to a customer and (b) sales and equivalent taxes collected for or on behalf of any governmental taxing authority.

tables.title: Your Estimated Initial Investment (Single Unit)
column_headers: To whom payment is to be made
rows: Real Estate and Facility Coordination Fee
Furniture, Fixtures, Classroom Equipment, and Decor (Note 3)
table_notes: Note 1: Each payment to us is non-refundable; third parties may have their own refund policies; franchisor does not finance any part of the initial investment.

Note 2: Estimates assume a tenant improvement allowance of $40 per square foot to retrofit 6,000 square feet; not every landlord will grant an allowance; assumes HVAC, plumbing, and electric wiring already completed; does not include site work, plan, government approvals, consents, entitlement costs, or cost to build/purchase a space.
Note 3: Classroom furniture from designated supplier; décor package purchased from franchisor; costs vary by school size; excludes transportation or set up.
Note 4: Outdoor playground equipment from approved supplier.
Note 5: Signage costs vary by façade size; includes wall signage, interior brand identification, and window graphics.
Note 6: Includes office computers, iPads, QuickBooks, and The Student Hub school-management and payment-processing software (see Item 11).
Note 7: Deposit equal to up to three months' rent; assumes free rent until School opens; purchasing real estate instead of renting would change estimates significantly.
Note 8: Based on historical experience of affiliate's School; varies by municipality and provider.
Note 9: Assumes payment of 25% of annual insurance cost before opening.
Note 10: Travel/lodging for up to three people to attend training in Charlotte, NC or another designated location.
Note 11: Minimum $20,000 grand opening marketing spend; all paid to approved supplier that manages and implements the marketing.

Note 12: Fees for initial reviews/advisory services of construction manager and architect; costs increase if additional accountant/attorney services or new construction bid; assumes no additional architect/civil engineer required under local laws.

Note 13: Three-month cash reserve to cover operations including payroll, inventory, rent; does not include finance charges, interest, or debt service; may need more or less depending on local conditions.

tables.title: Your Estimated Initial Investment — Multi-Unit Development Agreement
column_headers: To whom payment is to be made
table_notes: Fees payable at MUDA execution for two or three franchises.
Franchise fee is $35,000 for the second school and $25,000 for the third school.

Franchisee will also pay the $10,000 Real Estate and Facility Coordination Fee and $15,000 Training & Opening Support Fee for each additional school when the franchise agreement for that additional school is signed.
These fees are in addition to the initial investment to establish each school in the first Item 7 table.

tables.title: Training Program
rows: Phase 1: Business Startup and Real Estate
Online learning portal and in-person at Charlotte location
Online learning portal and in-person at Charlotte location
Online learning portal and in-person at Charlotte location
table_notes: Approximately 80 hours pre-opening training delivered virtually and at affiliate's School.
Two weeks of in-person training at a training-approved School required prior to opening; both an owner and either a staff representative or the School Administrator must complete it.
Training is typically conducted approximately 2 months before School opening.
Training materials include online materials, presentations, the Confidential Brand Standards Manual and related written materials.
Training program is led by Daniela McElhaney and Katherine Bell (15+ years with Ivybrook, 20+ years in child education industry).
Training and Opening Support Fee of $15,000 covers the initial training program.
Franchisee pays all travel costs and living expenses for attendees.
At least one owner and the School Administrator must satisfactorily complete training before opening date is approved.

tables.title: Brand Fund Expenditures (Percentage of Total Fund Expenditures)
table_notes: Brand Development Fund started July 1, 2018.
Franchised businesses contribute 1% of Gross Revenues monthly based on the previous month's revenues.
Brand Fund is administered by franchisor personnel; may be paid to a nonprofit entity in the future.
Brand Fund is not audited, though franchisor can require an audit.
Company-owned outlets are not required to contribute on the same basis as franchisees.
Other franchisees may contribute at a different rate than you.
Unspent Brand Fund monies are carried forward.

tables.title: Registered Trademarks
table_notes: Principal trademarks owned by Ivybrook Franchising, LLC.
Registered on the Principal Register at USPTO.
All required affidavits filed; all marks have been renewed.
No current material determinations or pending infringement, opposition, or cancellation proceedings known to franchisor.
Franchisor is not required to protect the Marks or defend franchisee.
Franchisor may require franchisee to modify or discontinue use of a trademark at franchisee expense.

tables.title: Registered Copyrights (Curriculum)
rows: Ivybrook Academy Music and Movement Curriculum
Ivybrook Academy Reading and Writing Labs
table_notes: Duration: 95 years from date of publication (approximately year 2102).
Affiliate does not intend to renew the copyrights.
No current material determinations or pending proceedings at USPTO/Copyright Office or courts.
Franchisor is not obligated to protect copyright or defend franchisee.

tables.title: The Franchise Relationship (Provisions a–w)
rows: b. Renewal or extension of term
Two additional terms of 10 years each

Sign successor franchise agreement for additional 10-year term; may have materially different terms; must have fully complied; pay renewal fee; maintain possession of Accepted Location or approved substitute; make capital expenditures for uniformity; pay all amounts due; not be in default; give timely written notice; sign current franchise agreement; comply with current training requirements; sign general release
Subject to state law, 30 days notice if franchisee in compliance, franchisor materially breaches, and fails to begin cure within 90 days of written notice
e. Termination by franchisor without cause
f. Termination by franchisor with cause
Failure to maintain insurance; failure to make payments; failure to comply with any mandatory specification, standard, or operating procedure; any other default not explicitly stated

Failure to select accepted site or begin operations; training failure; material misrepresentation; felony or conduct affecting reputation; unauthorized use of confidential information; failure to have owners/executives sign NDA/noncompetes; abandonment 3+ consecutive days; unauthorized transfer of control; failure to maintain supervision after death/disability; reports understating amounts due by more than 1% on 2+ occasions; bankruptcy/insolvency/general assignment; unauthorized Marks use; 2+ occasions within 12 months of failure to submit reports or pay fees; 2+ health/safety law violations or creating hazard; failure to comply with applicable law after notice; 2+ agreement/specification breaches within 12 months; cross-default; crisis notification violation; noncompete/non-solicit violation; operating without completing steps; unapproved trademark use; failing to meet minimum scores for customer evaluations or compliance; loss of required license; government non-compliance letters; failure to cure; incurable defaults. MUDA: failure to meet development schedule; violation of franchise or other agreement allowing termination

Stop operating; stop using trade secrets, confidential info, System and Marks; if requested, assign interest in franchise location; cancel/assign assumed names; pay all sums owed including damages and enforcement costs; return Brand Standards Manual and confidential info; turn over customer info; assign telephone number; comply with noncompetes, confidentiality, non-solicitation; follow instructions re technology/customer list/data; de-identify location; issue refunds upon direction; give franchisor right to lease location; pay liquidated damages

Sell, assign, convey, give away, pledge, mortgage, sublicense or otherwise transfer any interest in the franchise agreement, franchise, accepted location, assets, or ownership interest in the franchisee entity
No transfer without prior written consent

Franchisor has not exercised ROFR; all obligations paid; franchisee signs general release; transferee meets business/financial standards; transferee and owners sign then-current franchise agreement; provide copies of all transfer contracts; pay transfer fee and transfer training fee; transferee owners personally bound; third-party consents obtained; equity owners sign restrictive covenant agreements; transferee's owner and School Administrator complete initial training; transferee obtains insurance
Franchisor may match any offer for the Franchised Business or ownership interest
o. Franchisor option to purchase after termination
Not applicable to ongoing operations; during 60-day period after termination/non-renewal/expiration, franchisor may purchase any assets at fair market value
Interest must be transferred within 180 days of death or incapacity to approved transferee

All owners (and spouses/domestic partners) and designated others prohibited from diverting business/customers/suppliers, causing injury to Marks or System, or owning/working for a competitive business; subject to state law

2 years after termination/non-renewal/expiration: cannot own/work for competitive business within 10 miles of franchise location or the Territory (whichever is greater), or within 10 miles of any other Franchised Business; cannot solicit customers or suppliers; subject to state law
Subject to state law, only the terms of the franchise agreement (or MUDA) are binding; does not disclaim representations made in the FDD
Except for certain claims, all disputes must be first mediated and then arbitrated in the city of franchisor's principal place of business (currently Charlotte, NC)
Subject to state law, litigation must be pursued in courts in city of principal place of business (currently Charlotte, NC); most claims arbitrated in same city
Subject to state law, North Carolina law applies
table_notes: Additional disclosures required by certain states are in Exhibit B – Multi-State Addenda.

tables.title: TABLE 1: 2024 Revenue Stream Ranges
table_notes: Table presents low and high ranges for revenue streams available to the 24 franchisee-owned schools in Table 2B and one affiliate-owned school in Table 2A in 2024.
Each franchisee is free to set its own tuition prices.
Sample: 24 franchisee-owned schools + 1 affiliate-owned school.

tables.title: TABLE 2A: EBITDA of the Affiliate-Owned School (Jan 1 – Dec 31, 2024)
table_notes: Single affiliate-owned and operated school for period January 1, 2024 to December 31, 2024.
This school has seven classrooms.

Affiliate-owned school does not actually pay the 7% Royalty or the 1% Brand Fund Contribution; the figures shown for Royalty and Brand Fund are constructive — what a franchisee would have paid to achieve the affiliate's Gross Revenue.
Of the 24 franchisees in Table 2B, zero (0) achieved or surpassed the affiliate's Gross Revenue and two (2) achieved or surpassed the affiliate's EBITDA.
Note 3 Gross Revenue: all revenue received (tuition, grant, product sales) less sales and equivalent taxes.
Note 4 Labor Expenses: all staff and payroll expenses but does not include owners' salaries and benefits.
Note 5 Other Expenses: miscellaneous expenses such as inspections, security, licenses, décor, gifts, and company events.
Note 6 EBITDA: Gross Revenue minus Total Expenses but does not include deductions for interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization.

tables.title: TABLE 2B: EBITDA of Franchisees Open Two or More Years (opened before Jan 1, 2023)
table_notes: Sample: 24 franchisee-owned schools open two or more years (opened before January 1, 2023).
Number of 2Y+ franchisees who achieved or surpassed: (i) average Gross Revenues = 11; (ii) median Gross Revenues = 13; (iii) average EBITDA = 11; (iv) median EBITDA = 12.
Excludes schools not open two full years as of Dec 31, 2024, schools not in compliance with brand standards, and schools that ceased operations in 2024.
Franchisee-owned schools in this table opened between 2011 and 2022.

tables.title: TABLE 2C: EBITDA of Franchisees Open Three or More Years (opened before Jan 1, 2022)
table_notes: Sample: 18 franchisee-owned schools open three or more years (opened before January 1, 2022).
Number of 3Y+ franchisees who achieved or surpassed: (i) average Gross Revenues = 8; (ii) median Gross Revenues = 9; (iii) average EBITDA = 7; (iv) median EBITDA = 9.
Preserved contradiction: narrative text (p49) says Table 3 uses 19 schools open 3 Years +, while Table 2C header says 18. Both figures surfaced directly from the document.

The definition of Gross Revenues in the Item 19 notes differs from the Item 6 Note 2 definition; Item 19 defines Gross Revenues as 'all revenue received (tuition, grant revenue, product sales) less sales and equivalent taxes', while Item 6 defines it as 'all revenue generated ... including proceeds from business interruption insurance, excluding customer refunds and sales taxes.'

tables.title: TABLE 3: Gross Revenues by Classroom Count (3 Years+ Franchisees, 2024)
table_notes: Average and median Gross Revenues from January 1, 2024 to December 31, 2024 for franchisee-owned schools operating 3 Years +, categorized by number of classrooms.
Text states 'the 19 franchisee-owned schools who have been operating 3 Years +' but Table 2C describes the 3 Years+ cohort as 18 schools — unresolved internal inconsistency preserved.
Row totals: 4 + 13 + 2 = 19 schools.
The affiliate's school is a seven-classroom school and its results are disclosed in Table 2A (not in this Table 3).

Four states (Illinois, Michigan, Virginia, Wisconsin) are 'Pending' — note this FDD is filed with WI-DFI but Wisconsin is listed as Pending on this sheet.
